trigger: Traffic destined to an unresolved next-hop IP address causes the kernel to send an ARP request; kernel installs the resulting neighbor entry after receiving the ARP reply

steps.action: A packet arrives for an IP with no ARP entry; kernel sends ARP broadcast request on the egress interface and marks the neighbor as NUD_INCOMPLETE. On ARP reply receipt the neighbor transitions to NUD_REACHABLE and is installed in the kernel neighbor table.
steps.output: Kernel neighbor entry for the IP with resolved MAC address in state NUD_REACHABLE

steps.action: Receives RTM_NEWNEIGH netlink event from the kernel via libnl. NeighSync::onMsg() filters out NUD_INCOMPLETE/NUD_FAILED states (delete_key=true path) and, for valid NUD_REACHABLE entries, writes the neighbor (MAC + address family) to APPL_DB NEIGH_TABLE via m_neighTable.set().

ConsumerStateTable notifies NeighOrch of APPL_DB NEIGH_TABLE SET. NeighOrch::doTask() parses the interface and IP key, extracts the MAC from the 'neigh' field, then calls NeighOrch::addNeighbor() which invokes sai_neighbor_api->create_neighbor_entry() to create a SAI neighbor object. The entry is written to ASIC_DB.

Immediately after creating the SAI neighbor, NeighOrch::addNeighbor() calls NeighOrch::addNextHop() which invokes sai_next_hop_api->create_next_hop() with SAI_NEXT_HOP_TYPE_IP to create a SAI next-hop object referencing the resolved neighbor. The next-hop OID is stored in ASIC_DB and in the in-memory m_syncdNextHops map.

After neighbor and next-hop are created, NeighOrch::addNeighbor() calls notify(SUBJECT_TYPE_NEIGH_CHANGE, ...) to inform observers. RouteOrch (which has routes pending with this unresolved next-hop left in m_toSync) re-processes those routes on the next doTask() cycle: it now finds the next-hop OID in NeighOrch::hasNextHop() and successfully calls sai_route_api->create_route_entry(), writing the pending route to ASIC_DB.
steps.output: Routes that were blocked on this next-hop are now programmed; ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ROUTE_ENTRY entries reference the new next-hop OID
steps.verify: redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ROUTE_ENTRY*' | xargs -I{} redis-cli -n 1 HGET {} SAI_ROUTE_ENTRY_ATTR_NEXT_HOP_ID

steps.action: Receives SAI Redis notifications from ASIC_DB for the neighbor entry, next-hop, and any route updates via Syncd::processEvent() -> processSingleEvent(). For each CREATE command, dispatches to processQuadEvent(SAI_COMMON_API_CREATE, ...) which calls the vendor SAI library to program the MAC/IP binding, next-hop, and forwarding entries in the ASIC.
steps.output: Neighbor MAC binding and IP next-hop programmed in ASIC forwarding tables; traffic to the resolved IP now forwards at hardware speed

failure_injection_points.failure: neighsyncd not receiving kernel neighbor events
failure_injection_points.symptom: ip neigh show shows NUD_REACHABLE entry but redis-cli -n 0 KEYS 'NEIGH_TABLE:*' is empty for that IP
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check neighsyncd: docker exec swss supervisorctl status neighsyncd; check /var/log/swss/neighsyncd.log for netlink subscription errors; verify swss container is healthy

failure_injection_points.symptom: NEIGH_TABLE entry present in APPL_DB but no ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEIGHBOR_ENTRY in ASIC_DB
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check orchagent: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; grep 'NeighOrch' /var/log/swss/orchagent.log; confirm the interface has a router interface (rif_id) — orchagent skips neighbors on interfaces with no RIF

failure_injection_points.failure: SAI neighbor created but next-hop creation fails
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEIGHBOR_ENTRY exists but no corresponding SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_NEXT_HOP; routes pointing to this IP remain in APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE uninstalled
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check orchagent.log for 'Failed to create next hop'; look for SAI_STATUS_NO_MEMORY or resource exhaustion; check 'show system resources' for next-hop table utilization

failure_injection_points.failure: Route in hardware but next-hop unresolved (race or missed notification)
failure_injection_points.symptom: show ip route shows a route present, traffic blackholed; ASIC_DB has ROUTE_ENTRY but SAI_ROUTE_ENTRY_ATTR_NEXT_HOP_ID is SAI_NULL_OBJECT_ID or points to a trap/drop action
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: This occurs when RouteOrch programs a route before NeighOrch resolves the next-hop: RouteOrch::addRoute() calls m_neighOrch->resolveNeighbor() which writes to APPL_DB NEIGH_RESOLVE_TABLE; nbrmgrd picks this up via doResolveNeighTask() and sends RTM_NEWNEIGH to kernel to trigger ARP. Check 'redis-cli -n 0 KEYS NEIGH_RESOLVE_TABLE:*'; check /var/log/swss/orchagent.log for 'resolving neighbor'; verify kernel re-issues ARP with 'ip neigh flush <ip>' and retry

failure_injection_points.failure: syncd SAI call failing for neighbor or next-hop
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_DB entries present but syncd log shows SAI_STATUS_FAILURE; traffic not forwarding despite orchagent success
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: Check syncd: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd; grep 'SAI_STATUS' /var/log/syncd.log; look for vendor-specific SAI errors; check if neighbor table (ARP/NDP cache) in ASIC is full
